title: leucanthemum vulgare subsp. subalpinum
intro: Leucanthemum vulgare subsp. subalpinum, also known as the alpine daisy or mountain daisy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of wounds and skin irritations due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Leucanthemum vulgare subsp. subalpinum include its ability to soothe and calm skin conditions, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Leucanthemum vulgare subsp. subalpinum can be prepared as a herbal remedy in the form of infusions, decoctions, or salves, which can be used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
